title: vSAN Disk Group Health
spl:
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-4h
| where state!="healthy" OR checksum_errors>0 OR component_state!="active"
| stats latest(state) as st, sum(checksum_errors) as checksums by cluster, host, dg_name
| sort -checksums
description: Disk group mount state, component health, and checksum errors for VMware vSAN.
value: Disk group mount state, component health, and checksum errors for VMware vSAN.
implementation: Ingest vSAN health JSON. Page on unhealthy disk group or rising checksums. Correlate with physical disk (UC-19.2.5).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: vSAN health API, vCenter TA.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `vsan:diskgroup`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest vSAN health JSON. Page on unhealthy disk group or rising checksums. Correlate with physical disk (UC-19.2.5).

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hci sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup" earliest=-4h
| where state!="healthy" OR checksum_errors>0 OR component_state!="active"
| stats latest(state) as st, sum(checksum_errors) as checksums by cluster, host, dg_name
| sort -checksums
```

Understanding this SPL

**vSAN Disk Group Health** ‚Äî Disk group mount state, component health, and checksum errors for VMware vSAN.

Documented **Data sources**: `vsan:diskgroup`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): vSAN health API, vCenter TA. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hci; **sourcetype**: vsan:diskgroup. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=hci, sourcetype="vsan:diskgroup", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where state!="healthy" OR checksum_errors>0 OR component_state!="active"` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cluster, host, dg_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Compare with vSphere and vSAN health UIs, Skyline, or VMware vCenter for the same objects and time window.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Status grid (DG √ó host), Table (issues), Timeline (events).
grandmaExplanation: Disk group mount state, component health, and checksum errors for VMware vSAN ‚Äî so hardware faults and capacity problems do not take services down.
knownFalsePositives: Cluster events during planned hardware refresh, capacity adds, or scheduled maintenance modes.
